author: nero_eth
content: We do yeah. <@508125616940515329> can provide further details but in short, transactions are crafted to have dependencies such that they must be executed sequentially with today's clients.

author: jochembrouwer
content:
The BAL-specific benchmarks are created such that they are not able to be executed in parallel without the BAL. One example is a hash chain where tx performs SLOAD(0) and then keeps KECCAK256 this, and with the final gas remaining to execute it, SSTORE(0, result). The next tx will do the same. 
This is not possible to optimize otherwise you would have found an optimization to speed up hash calculations.
Relevant PR with these kind of BAL optimization targets: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2197

author: jochembrouwer
content: (of course, with BAL, the intermediate results are now in BAL and you thus only have to verify the short sequences to see if the hash chain ends up calculating the same hash as was provided in BAL -> use the hash in BAL to start any hash chain from any later tx, thus you can now parallelize it)
